Description: Lower bound (2.5% quantile) of the Bayesian credible interval for the 3-month forward beta change forecast
2.5% quantile (lower bound) of the Bayesian posterior predictive distribution for the 3-month forward beta change; represents the lower end of the 95% credible interval
Dispersion (spread) of the Bayesian predictive distribution for 3-month forward beta change, reflecting variability and shape beyond standard deviation

Posterior dispersion (spread) metric of the forecasted 3-month forward beta change, measuring the width or variability of the Bayesian predictive distribution, reflecting uncertainty and tail risk in the beta change forecast
Posterior standard deviation (uncertainty) of the forecasted beta change from the Bayesian model, indicating confidence in the beta change prediction
Posterior standard deviation (uncertainty) of the Bayesian model's forecast for 3-month forward beta change; quantifies how uncertain the model is about its beta change prediction
Normalized Bayesian forecast probability that the future 3-month beta will have a risk surprise (substantial deviation outside forecast credible interval)

Forecasted Bayesian probability (posterior mean) that the realized 3-month forward beta will experience a risk surprise, i.e., a substantial deviation from the credible interval; normalized probability of risk event
Upper bound (97.5% quantile) of the Bayesian credible interval for the 3-month forward beta change forecast
97.5% quantile (upper bound) of the Bayesian posterior predictive distribution for the 3-month forward beta change; represents the upper end of the 95% credible interval
Volatility of the stock’s CAPM model residuals (idiosyncratic volatility) over the past 252 trading days
Idiosyncratic volatility measured as the standard deviation of CAPM model residuals over the last 252 trading days; represents risk not explained by the market factor
Beta of the stock to the market, calculated using only days when the market performed below its 252-day moving average, indicating downside sensitivity to market downturns
CAPM beta calculated using only those days when the market index return is below its 252-day average, capturing sensitivity to market downturns
Lower tail dependence between the stock and market, measuring the probability of joint extreme negative moves, calculated using a rotated Gumbel copula

Lower tail dependence between the stock return and the market index, estimated using a rotated Gumbel copula; quantifies joint downside risk or the probability of both stock and market experiencing extreme negative returns
Classic CAPM beta (possibly with shrinkage applied), representing the stock’s historical sensitivity to market returns using covariance and variance
Classical CAPM beta (possibly tail-shrunk); the stock's sensitivity to market returns, calculated as the covariance of stock return and market index return divided by the market index variance
Empirical realized volatility of the stock, calculated as the standard deviation of daily returns over the last 252 trading days
Standard deviation of daily stock returns computed over the past 252 trading days (about 1 year); empirical historical volatility of the stock
Market capitalization of the company, usually in USD, representing the total market value of a company's outstanding shares
Market capitalization of the stock, typically in units of US dollars, representing the company's total equity value
Standard deviation of the residuals (idiosyncratic volatility) from the Wolfe QES multifactor risk model over past 252 trading days
Standard deviation of regression residuals (idiosyncratic volatility) from Wolfe's QES multifactor risk model over past 252 days; captures risk not explained by systematic factors
Point forecast of the 3-month forward change in market beta for the stock, estimated by a Bayesian model
Point forecast (posterior mean) of the 3-month forward change in market beta as predicted by the Bayesian model
Systematic beta of the stock estimated by Wolfe’s multifactor QES risk model (“US All Cap Model”), reflecting sensitivity to systematic risk factors
Systematic beta, estimated via Wolfe’s multifactor QES US All Cap Model, representing market sensitivity derived from the model
